betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The §6Control Circuit Board§f (advancedrocketry:ic:3) is the §6most-produced circuit board§f in the AR progression — it backs every AR machine's controller and is the §6marker block§f that defines a multiblock's controller face. Without it, no AR machine can be completed.

§6Production Path (controlcircuitboard.json)§f:
- Type: §6minecraft:crafting_shaped§f
- Pattern (3×3):
  ```
  r v r
  d w d
  d p d
  ```
- Key: `r`=§6dustRedstone§f (forge:ore_dict), `v`=§6gemQuartz§f (forge:ore_dict, Nether Quartz), `d`=§6dustCopper§f (forge:ore_dict), `w`=§6slabWood§f (forge:ore_dict), `p`=§6plateIron§f (forge:ore_dict)
- Output: 1x §6Control Circuit Board§f (advancedrocketry:ic:3)
- Power: passive; the recipe runs in any §6vanilla crafting table§f

The control circuit board is unique among AR circuits because it has a §6vanilla crafting table§f recipe — it does not require a multiblock. This makes it the §6first circuit board§f the player can produce, and the §6fastest§f. The board is used as the §6controller marker§f in every AR multiblock — the player right-clicks the control circuit with a §6Wrench§f to assemble the multiblock preview.

The control circuit board is a prerequisite for every AR multiblock machine (Lathe, Rolling Machine, Centrifuge, etc.) and the §6Satellite Builder§f (10039), the §6Guidance Computer Access Hatch§f (10047), and the §6Warp Controller§f (10038). It is consumed at a rate of 1 per machine built, so AR players routinely need 50+ in storage.

§eTip§f: The control circuit board's vanilla recipe makes it the §6easiest circuit§f to mass-produce. Set up an §6autocrafting§f setup with §6AE2§f or §6Refined Storage§f to keep a steady supply — running out mid-multiblock-build forces you to disassemble and rebuild. A stack of 64 fits comfortably in a single §6Drawers§f unit for quick access.
